analysis_description: Merged origin/main for Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 (days 1–3) and round4work/. Ran Phase 1 pipeline `r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py`: merges trades with BBO at trade timestamp, infers aggressor (price>=ask aggr_buy, price<=bid aggr_sell), forward mid = mid(ts+K*100)−mid(ts) for K∈{5,20,100} on the 100-tick grid; spread_regime = tight if TOB spread ≤ day×symbol median else wide; burst = ≥2 symbols same (day,timestamp). Cross-asset fwd columns for VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT and HYDROGEL_PACK in events CSV.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate satisfied in `round4_phase1_complete`. Note: `TradingState.market_trades` is never populated in prosperity4bt test_runner (always {}), so live-strategy counterparty hooks need tape CSV ingestion or an engine change.

bullet_summaries.conclusion: Strongest pattern: aggressive BUYs on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT when Mark 22 or Mark 49 is seller show mean forward extract mid +~1.8 to +2.1 at K=5 with high t-stat; Mark 67 as buyer similar. Per-day means for Mark67/Mark22 extract K=5 stay positive on days 1–3.

bullet_summaries.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (cell means + residuals)
bullet_summaries.conclusion: Residuals vs (buyer,seller,symbol,regime,K) cell means show Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 has negative LOO residuals on held-out day 1 at K=5/20; Mark 14→Mark 38 hydro stays positive — structured basket residuals on the 01→22 pipe.
Directed graph is sparse (19 edges); Mark 14 is count hub; Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates voucher basket prints; consecutive A→B→C chains reflect same routing.
Multi-symbol same-timestamp bursts vs random timestamps: extract forward mean at K=20 is higher on bursts (~0.22 vs ~0.03); K=5 similar — burst signal is longer horizon.
Ranked mean fwd_same at K=20 by (buyer,seller) under aggr_buy/aggr_sell flags pairs with worst markouts for passive liquidity (see CSV).

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: After aggressive BUY on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT with Mark 22 as seller, extract mid rises ~+2 ticks by K=5 on average
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean fwd_same ≈ +2.0 at K=5; per-day mean +1.8–+2.3 on days 1–3
top_5_tradeable_edges.n: ~86 prints pooled; validate in sim with worse fills before sizing

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: Same with Mark 49 as passive seller on aggressive extract buys
Mark 67 as buyer on aggressive extract buys — forward extract drift
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean ≈ +2.0 at K=5; all three days

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: Multi-symbol same-timestamp burst → higher extract forward at K=20 vs random time control
Mark 22 as seller on aggressive SELLs on VEV_5300 during bursts — negative short-horizon same-symbol fwd (fade candidate)

negative_or_null_findings: K=5 extract forward after bursts is not meaningfully different from random controls; burst signal mainly at K=20.
Most per-participant × VEV cells have too few prints for stable inference outside extract and 01→22 lanes.
Backtester `TradingState.market_trades` is empty during `Trader.run` — tape CSV is authoritative for Mark conditioning until runner exposes trades.
round4work/round4description.txt TTE: only VEV_5000 example; `r4_t_years.py` uses 5−day at open + R3-style intraday wind (confirm if spec updates).

analysis_description: Phase 2 script `r4_phase2_analysis.py`: (1) named-bot burst window ±5 ticks around Mark01→Mark22 timestamps — VEV_5300 fwd5/fwd20 MR fraction ~0.14 (not a clean fade); (2) microstructure — extract spread quartiles vs |Δmid|5 are flat (`extract_spread_quartile_vs_fwd_vol.csv`); joint gate vs short vol shows only day-3 slightly lower vol when tight; (3) lead–lag signed product Δ×lag: VEV_5200/5300 contemporaneous with extract dominates (`leadlag_best_lag_by_day.csv`); hydro weak negative cross at odd lags; (4) Phase-1 extract aggr_buy×Mark22/49 split by **Sonic joint gate** (`phase1_extract_signal_by_joint_gate.csv`) — **refines** Phase-1: when gate tight, day-1 Mark22 mean +0.75 on n=4 (tiny n); pooled pattern is still buyer-side drift but gate slices are count-starved; (5) rough IV residual Mark01→22 by VEV (`iv_residual_*`); (6) worst markouts tail from Phase-1 CSV (`worst_markouts_k20_tail.csv`); (7) Mark55 rolling flow terciles vs extract fwd5 (`mark55_flow_imbalance_vs_extract_fwd5.csv`) — mixed by day.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate in `round4_phase2_complete`. `trader_v1.py` implements orthogonal live microstructure + joint gate (sim); counterparty edges remain tape-only until engine exposes trades.

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Phase-1 aggressive extract buy + Mark22/49 seller **still positive** in wide-book majority of prints; conditioning on **Sonic joint tight** yields **very small n** (e.g. day-1 Mark22 n=4, mean +0.75) — does **not** refute Phase-1 but shows the tape edge is **not** cleanly gated by 5200+5300≤2 on this slice (often opposite sign / low count). Phase-1 burst K=20 lift **confirmed** orthogonal (micro vol vs burst timing). Mark 01→22 VEV_5300 near-burst **does not** show high mean-revert rate (~14%).

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Contemporaneous co-move: extract tick Δ aligns with VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 tick Δ (same timestamp); strongest cross-moment at lag 0 all three days
Avoid passive liquidity in worst tail: aggr_sell Mark49→Mark22 and Mark22→Mark38 show large negative k20 fwd on printed symbol
Extract-only sim with joint gate + microprice skew — positive PnL vs v0 zero (sanity that micro+gate can be wired live)
Joint tight vs wide: slightly lower short-horizon extract |Δmid| on day 3 when gate tight (regime split)
Rough IV residual at Mark01→Mark22 VEV prints — strike-level skew vs ATM IV (for future smile/MM); file iv_residual_mark01_mark22_by_symbol.csv

phase2_negative: Extract TOB spread quartile vs forward |Δmid|5 is flat — no spread-compression directional vol edge in this binning.
Burst-window (±5 ticks of 01→22) VEV_5300: mean-revert fraction only ~14%; not a standalone fade rule.
Mark55 signed-flow terciles vs extract fwd5 flip sign by day — weak inventory signal without more structure.
